title: Handle BGG HTTP 202 'request queued' on the collection endpoint (Greptile P1)
rationale: BGG's XMLAPI2 collection endpoint returns HTTP 202 with a '<message>Your request for this collection has been accepted and will be processed. Please try again later.</message>' body whenever the server queues a large collection lookup. The client's success branch treats any status < 400 as data, so the 202 placeholder body was passed through XMLToJSON and surfaced to the user as if it were valid collection data.

changes.summary: Added an explicit HTTP 202 handler before the success branch in do(): poll with exponential backoff (same pattern as the 429/500 retries) until the queued result is ready, and return a clear APIError after retries are exhausted instead of surfacing the placeholder body as collection data. Matches BGG's documented try-again-later behavior.
changes.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; 202 responses retry instead of returning the 'request accepted' message as data.
